title: betaDelta: Confidence Intervals for Standardized Regression Coefficients
text: Generates confidence intervals standardized regression coefficients using delta method standard errors models fitted lm() described Yuan Chan (2011: http://doi.org/10.1007/s11336-011-9224-6) Jones Waller (2015: http://doi.org/10.1007/s11336-013-9380-y).

title: betaDelta: Confidence Intervals for Standardized Regression Coefficients
text: can install CRAN release betaDelta : can install development version betaDelta GitHub :
code: install.packages("betaDelta") if (!require("remotes")) install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("jeksterslab/betaDelta")

title: betaDelta: Confidence Intervals for Standardized Regression Coefficients
text: Jones, J. ., & Waller, N. G. (2015). normal-theory asymptotic distribution-free (ADF) covariance matrix standardized regression coefficients: Theoretical extensions finite sample behavior. Psychometrika, 80(2), 365–378. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11336-013-9380-y Yuan, K.-H., & Chan, W. (2011). Biases standard errors standardized regression coefficients. Psychometrika, 76(4), 670–690. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11336-011-9224-6

what: Fit the regression model using the lm() function.
title: betaDelta: Example Using the BetaDelta Function
code: object <- lm(QUALITY ~ NARTIC + PCTGRT + PCTSUPP, data = df)

betaDelta: Example Using the BetaDelta Function
code: mvn <- BetaDelta(object, type = "mvn") adf <- BetaDelta(object, type = "adf")

title: betaDelta: Example Using the BetaDelta Function
text: Summary results BetaDelta().

title: betaDelta: Example Using the BetaDelta Function
text: Calculate standardized regression slopes.
code: coef(mvn) #>    NARTIC    PCTGRT   PCTSUPP  #> 0.4951451 0.3914887 0.2632477 coef(adf) #>    NARTIC    PCTGRT   PCTSUPP  #> 0.4951451 0.3914887 0.2632477

previous_headings: Estimate the standardized regression slopes and the corresponding sampling covariance matrix.

what: Estimate differences of standardized regression slopes and the corresponding sampling covariance matrix.
title: betaDelta: Example Using the DiffBetaDelta Function
code: mvn <- DiffBetaDelta(std_mvn) adf <- DiffBetaDelta(std_adf)

previous_headings: Estimate differences of standardized regression slopes and the corresponding sampling covariance matrix.
title: betaDelta: Example Using the DiffBetaDelta Function
text: Summary results DiffBetaDelta().
code: summary(mvn) #> Difference between standardized regression coefficients with MVN standard errors: #>                   est     se      t      p   0.05%    0.5%    2.5%  97.5% #> NARTIC-PCTGRT  0.1037 0.1357 0.7640 0.4491 -0.3763 -0.2624 -0.1702 0.3775 #> NARTIC-PCTSUPP 0.2319 0.1252 1.8524 0.0710 -0.2110 -0.1059 -0.0207 0.4845 #> PCTGRT-PCTSUPP 0.1282 0.1227 1.0451 0.3020 -0.3059 -0.2028 -0.1194 0.3759 #>                 99.5% 99.95% #> NARTIC-PCTGRT  0.4697 0.5837 #> NARTIC-PCTSUPP 0.5697 0.6748 #> PCTGRT-PCTSUPP 0.4593 0.5624 summary(adf) #> Difference between standardized regression coefficients with ADF standard errors: #>                   est     se      t      p   0.05%    0.5%    2.5%  97.5% #> NARTIC-PCTGRT  0.1037 0.1212 0.8555 0.3971 -0.3250 -0.2233 -0.1409 0.3482 #> NARTIC-PCTSUPP 0.2319 0.1181 1.9642 0.0561 -0.1858 -0.0866 -0.0064 0.4702 #> PCTGRT-PCTSUPP 0.1282 0.1215 1.0555 0.2973 -0.3016 -0.1996 -0.1170 0.3734 #>                 99.5% 99.95% #> NARTIC-PCTGRT  0.4306 0.5323 #> NARTIC-PCTSUPP 0.5504 0.6496 #> PCTGRT-PCTSUPP 0.4561 0.5581

previous_headings: Estimate differences of standardized regression slopes and the corresponding sampling covariance matrix.
title: betaDelta: Example Using the DiffBetaDelta Function
text: Calculate differences standardized regression slopes.
code: coef(mvn) #>  NARTIC-PCTGRT NARTIC-PCTSUPP PCTGRT-PCTSUPP  #>      0.1036564      0.2318974      0.1282410 coef(adf) #>  NARTIC-PCTGRT NARTIC-PCTSUPP PCTGRT-PCTSUPP  #>      0.1036564      0.2318974      0.1282410

previous_headings: Estimate differences of standardized regression slopes and the corresponding sampling covariance matrix.
title: betaDelta: Example Using the DiffBetaDelta Function
text: Calculate sampling covariance matrix differences standardized regression slopes.
code: vcov(mvn) #>                NARTIC-PCTGRT NARTIC-PCTSUPP PCTGRT-PCTSUPP #> NARTIC-PCTGRT    0.018408653    0.009511262   -0.008897391 #> NARTIC-PCTSUPP   0.009511262    0.015672035    0.006160773 #> PCTGRT-PCTSUPP  -0.008897391    0.006160773    0.015058164 vcov(adf) #>                NARTIC-PCTGRT NARTIC-PCTSUPP PCTGRT-PCTSUPP #> NARTIC-PCTGRT    0.014681407    0.006928651   -0.007752755 #> NARTIC-PCTSUPP   0.006928651    0.013938955    0.007010303 #> PCTGRT-PCTSUPP  -0.007752755    0.007010303    0.014763058

previous_headings: Estimate differences of standardized regression slopes and the corresponding sampling covariance matrix.

title: Confidence Intervals for Standardized Regression Coefficients
text: Generates confidence intervals standardized regression coefficients using delta method standard errors models fitted lm() described Yuan Chan (2011: http://doi.org/10.1007/s11336-011-9224-6) Jones Waller (2015: http://doi.org/10.1007/s11336-013-9380-y).

title: Confidence Intervals for Standardized Regression Coefficients
text: can install CRAN release betaDelta : can install development version betaDelta GitHub :
code: install.packages("betaDelta") if (!require("remotes")) install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("jeksterslab/betaDelta")

title: Confidence Intervals for Standardized Regression Coefficients
text: example, multiple regression model fitted using program quality ratings (QUALITY) regressand/outcome variable number published articles attributed program faculty members (NARTIC), percent faculty members holding research grants (PCTGRT), percentage program graduates received support (PCTSUPP) regressor/predictor variables using data set 1982 ratings 46 doctoral programs psychology USA (National Research Council, 1982). Confidence intervals standardized regression coefficients generated using BetaDelta() function betaDelta package following Yuan Chan (2011) Jones Waller (2015).
code: library(betaDelta) df <- betaDelta::nas1982

what: Fit the regression model using the lm() function.
title: Confidence Intervals for Standardized Regression Coefficients
code: object <- lm(QUALITY ~ NARTIC + PCTGRT + PCTSUPP, data = df)

Estimate Standardized Regression Coefficients

title:
Estimate Standardized Regression Coefficients
and the Corresponding Sampling Covariance Matrix — BetaDelta
text: object Object class lm. type Character string. type = "mvn", use multivariate normal-theory approach. type = "adf", use asymptotic distribution-free approach.

title:
Estimate Standardized Regression Coefficients
and the Corresponding Sampling Covariance Matrix — BetaDelta
text: Returns object class betadelta list following elements: call Function call. args Function arguments. lm_process Processed lm object. gamma Asymptotic covariance matrix sample covariance matrix. acov Asymptotic covariance matrix standardized slopes. vcov Sampling covariance matrix standardized slopes. est Vector standardized slopes.

title:

and the Corresponding Sampling Covariance Matrix — BetaDelta
text: Jones, J. ., & Waller, N. G. (2015). normal-theory asymptotic distribution-free (ADF) covariance matrix standardized regression coefficients: Theoretical extensions finite sample behavior. Psychometrika, 80(2), 365–378. doi:10.1007/s11336-013-9380-y Yuan, K.-H., & Chan, W. (2011). Biases standard errors standardized regression coefficients. Psychometrika, 76(4), 670–690. doi:10.1007/s11336-011-9224-6

title:
Estimate Differences of Standardized Slopes

text: object Object class betadelta, , output BetaDelta() function.

title:
Estimate Differences of Standardized Slopes
and the Corresponding Sampling Covariance Matrix — DiffBetaDelta
text: Returns object class diffbetadelta list following elements: fit argument object. vcov Sampling covariance matrix differences standardized slopes. est Vector differences standardized slopes.

title:
Estimate Differences of Standardized Slopes
and the Corresponding Sampling Covariance Matrix — DiffBetaDelta
text: Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan

title:
Estimate Differences of Standardized Slopes
and the Corresponding Sampling Covariance Matrix — DiffBetaDelta
code: object <- lm(QUALITY ~ NARTIC + PCTGRT + PCTSUPP, data = nas1982) std <- BetaDelta(object) diff <- DiffBetaDelta(std) # Methods ------------------------------------------------------- print(diff) #> Difference between standardized regression coefficients with MVN standard errors: #>                   est     se      t      p   0.05%    0.5%    2.5%  97.5% #> NARTIC-PCTGRT  0.1037 0.1357 0.7640 0.4491 -0.3763 -0.2624 -0.1702 0.3775 #> NARTIC-PCTSUPP 0.2319 0.1252 1.8524 0.0710 -0.2110 -0.1059 -0.0207 0.4845 #> PCTGRT-PCTSUPP 0.1282 0.1227 1.0451 0.3020 -0.3059 -0.2028 -0.1194 0.3759 #>                 99.5% 99.95% #> NARTIC-PCTGRT  0.4697 0.5837 #> NARTIC-PCTSUPP 0.5697 0.6748 #> PCTGRT-PCTSUPP 0.4593 0.5624 summary(diff) #> Difference between standardized regression coefficients with MVN standard errors: #>                   est     se      t      p   0.05%    0.5%    2.5%  97.5% #> NARTIC-PCTGRT  0.1037 0.1357 0.7640 0.4491 -0.3763 -0.2624 -0.1702 0.3775 #> NARTIC-PCTSUPP 0.2319 0.1252 1.8524 0.0710 -0.2110 -0.1059 -0.0207 0.4845 #> PCTGRT-PCTSUPP 0.1282 0.1227 1.0451 0.3020 -0.3059 -0.2028 -0.1194 0.3759 #>                 99.5% 99.95% #> NARTIC-PCTGRT  0.4697 0.5837 #> NARTIC-PCTSUPP 0.5697 0.6748 #> PCTGRT-PCTSUPP 0.4593 0.5624 coef(diff) #>  NARTIC-PCTGRT NARTIC-PCTSUPP PCTGRT-PCTSUPP  #>      0.1036564      0.2318974      0.1282410  vcov(diff) #>                NARTIC-PCTGRT NARTIC-PCTSUPP PCTGRT-PCTSUPP #> NARTIC-PCTGRT    0.018408653    0.009511262   -0.008897391 #> NARTIC-PCTSUPP   0.009511262    0.015672035    0.006160773 #> PCTGRT-PCTSUPP  -0.008897391    0.006160773    0.015058164 confint(diff, level = 0.95) #>                       2.5%     97.5% #> NARTIC-PCTGRT  -0.17015387 0.3774667 #> NARTIC-PCTSUPP -0.02074216 0.4845371 #> PCTGRT-PCTSUPP -0.11940123 0.3758833

title: Confidence Intervals for Standardized Regression Slopes — confint.betadelta
text: object Object class betadelta. parm specification parameters given confidence intervals, either vector numbers vector names. missing, parameters considered. level confidence level required. ... additional arguments.

title: Confidence Intervals for Standardized Regression Slopes — confint.betadelta
text: Returns matrix confidence intervals.

title: Confidence Intervals for Standardized Regression Slopes — confint.betadelta
text: Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan

title: Confidence Intervals for Standardized Regression Slopes — confint.betadelta
code: object <- lm(QUALITY ~ NARTIC + PCTGRT + PCTSUPP, data = nas1982) std <- BetaDelta(object) confint(std, level = 0.95) #>              2.5%     97.5% #> NARTIC  0.3420563 0.6482339 #> PCTGRT  0.2360380 0.5469395 #> PCTSUPP 0.1124272 0.4140682

title: 1982 National Academy of Sciences Doctoral Programs Data — nas1982
text: Ratings 46 doctoral programs psychology USA following variables: QUALITY Program quality ratings. NFACUL Number faculty members program. NGRADS Number program graduates. PCTSUPP Percentage program graduates received support. PCTGRT Percent faculty members holding research grants. NARTIC Number published articles attributed program faculty member. PCTPUB Percent faculty one published article.

title: 1982 National Academy of Sciences Doctoral Programs Data — nas1982
text: National Research Council. (1982). assessment research-doctorate programs United States: Social behavioral sciences. doi:10.17226/9781 . Reproduced permission National Academy Sciences, Courtesy National Academies Press, Washington, D.C.

title: Print Method for an Object of Class betadelta — print.betadelta
text: x Object class betadelta. alpha Significance level. digits Digits print. ... additional arguments.

title: Print Method for an Object of Class betadelta — print.betadelta
text: Returns matrix standardized regression slopes, standard errors, test statistics, p-values, confidence intervals.

title: Print Method for an Object of Class betadelta — print.betadelta
text: Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan

title: Print Method for an Object of Class betadelta — print.betadelta
code: object <- lm(QUALITY ~ NARTIC + PCTGRT + PCTSUPP, data = nas1982) std <- BetaDelta(object) print(std) #> Call: #> BetaDelta(object = object) #>  #> Standardized regression slopes with MVN standard errors: #>            est     se      t     p   0.05%   0.5%   2.5%  97.5%  99.5% 99.95% #> NARTIC  0.4951 0.0759 6.5272 0.000  0.2268 0.2905 0.3421 0.6482 0.6998 0.7635 #> PCTGRT  0.3915 0.0770 5.0824 0.000  0.1190 0.1837 0.2360 0.5469 0.5993 0.6640 #> PCTSUPP 0.2632 0.0747 3.5224 0.001 -0.0011 0.0616 0.1124 0.4141 0.4649 0.5276

title: Summary Method for an Object of Class betadelta — summary.betadelta
code: object <- lm(QUALITY ~ NARTIC + PCTGRT + PCTSUPP, data = nas1982) std <- BetaDelta(object) summary(std) #> Call: #> BetaDelta(object = object) #>  #> Standardized regression slopes with MVN standard errors: #>            est     se      t     p   0.05%   0.5%   2.5%  97.5%  99.5% 99.95% #> NARTIC  0.4951 0.0759 6.5272 0.000  0.2268 0.2905 0.3421 0.6482 0.6998 0.7635 #> PCTGRT  0.3915 0.0770 5.0824 0.000  0.1190 0.1837 0.2360 0.5469 0.5993 0.6640 #> PCTSUPP 0.2632 0.0747 3.5224 0.001 -0.0011 0.0616 0.1124 0.4141 0.4649 0.5276

title: Summary Method for an Object of Class diffbetadelta — summary.diffbetadelta
text: Returns matrix standardized regression slopes, standard errors, test statistics, p-values, confidence intervals.

title: Summary Method for an Object of Class diffbetadelta — summary.diffbetadelta
text: Ivan Jacob Agaloos Pesigan

title: Summary Method for an Object of Class diffbetadelta — summary.diffbetadelta
code: object <- lm(QUALITY ~ NARTIC + PCTGRT + PCTSUPP, data = nas1982) std <- BetaDelta(object) diff <- DiffBetaDelta(std) summary(diff) #> Difference between standardized regression coefficients with MVN standard errors: #>                   est     se      t      p   0.05%    0.5%    2.5%  97.5% #> NARTIC-PCTGRT  0.1037 0.1357 0.7640 0.4491 -0.3763 -0.2624 -0.1702 0.3775 #> NARTIC-PCTSUPP 0.2319 0.1252 1.8524 0.0710 -0.2110 -0.1059 -0.0207 0.4845 #> PCTGRT-PCTSUPP 0.1282 0.1227 1.0451 0.3020 -0.3059 -0.2028 -0.1194 0.3759 #>                 99.5% 99.95% #> NARTIC-PCTGRT  0.4697 0.5837 #> NARTIC-PCTSUPP 0.5697 0.6748 #> PCTGRT-PCTSUPP 0.4593 0.5624
